heading: Discrimination prohibited if based on race, religion, national origin, or sex
label: 2755 Discrimination prohibited if based on race, religion, national origin, or sex

children.heading: Congressional declaration of policy
children.label: a Congressional declaration of policy
children.text: It is the policy of the United States that no sales should be made, and no credits (including participations in credits) or guaranties extended to or for any foreign country, the laws, regulations, official policies, or governmental practices of which prevent any United States person (as defined in ) from participating in the furnishing of defense articles or defense services under this chapter on the basis of race, religion, national origin, or sex.section 7701(a)(30) of title 26

children.heading: Employment of personnel; required contractual provision
children.label: b Employment of personnel; required contractual provision

children.text: No agency performing functions under this chapter shall, in employing or assigning personnel to participate in the performance of any such function, whether in the United States or abroad, take into account the exclusionary policies or practices of any foreign government where such policies or practices are based upon race, religion, national origin, or sex.

Each contract entered into by any such agency for the performance of any function under this chapter shall contain a provision to the effect that no person, partnership, corporation, or other entity performing functions pursuant to such contract, shall, in employing or assigning personnel to participate in the performance of any such function, whether in the United States or abroad, take into account the exclusionary policies or practices of any foreign government where such policies or practices are based upon race, religion, national origin, or sex.

children.heading: Report by President; contents
children.label: c Report by President; contents
children.text: The President shall promptly transmit reports to the Speaker of the House of Representatives, the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives, and the chairman of the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate concerning any instance in which any United States person (as defined in ) is prevented by a foreign government on the basis of race, religion, national origin, or sex, from participating in the performance of any sale or licensed transaction under this chapter or any import or export under a treaty referred to in . Such reports shall include (1) a description of the facts and circumstances of any such discrimination, (2) the response thereto on the part of the United States or any agency or employee thereof, and (3) the result of such response, if any.section 7701(a)(30) of title 26section 2778(j)(1)(C)(i) of this title

children.heading: Congressional request for information from President; information required; 60 day period; failure to supply information; termination or restriction of sale
children.label: d Congressional request for information from President; information required; 60 day period; failure to supply information; termination or restriction of sale
A
children.text: all the available information about the exclusionary policies or practices of the government of such country when such policies or practices are based upon race, religion, national origin or sex and prevent any such person from participating in the performance of any sale or licensed transaction under this chapter;

children.label: B
children.text: the response of the United States thereto and the results of such response;

children.label: i
children.text: extraordinary circumstances exist which necessitate a continuation of such sale or licensed transaction, and, if so, a description of such circumstances and the extent to which such sale or licensed transaction should be continued (subject to such conditions as Congress may impose under this section), and

children.label: ii
children.text: on all the facts it is in the national interest of the United States to continue such sale or licensed transaction; and

children.label: D
children.text: such other information as such committee may request.

In the event a statement with respect to a sale or licensed transaction is requested pursuant to paragraph (1) of this subsection but is not transmitted in accordance therewith within 60 days after receipt of such request, such sale or licensed transaction shall be suspended unless and until such statement is transmitted.

children.label: A
children.text: In the event a statement with respect to a sale or licensed transaction is transmitted under paragraph (1) of this subsection, the Congress may at any time thereafter adopt a joint resolution terminating or restricting such sale or licensed transaction.

children.label: B
children.text: Any such resolution shall be considered in the Senate in accordance with the provisions of section 601(b) of the International Security Assistance and Arms Export Control Act of 1976.

children.label: C
children.text: The term “certification”, as used in section 601 of such Act, means, for the purposes of this paragraph, a statement transmitted under paragraph (1) of this subsection.
